already been applied by the University pursuant to Schedule 1 from the date of the Student’s termination; and  
(c) submit to [redacted] a report on the work completed to date.  

2.3.6 The University must use reasonable endeavours to ensure the Student:  
(a) commences the Project on the Start Date and diligently undertakes the Project to the standards and levels of competence expected of a student on the Course;  
(b) performs the research required for the Project and delivers that research in a manner and in accordance with the Milestones and applicable due dates specified in Schedule 1;  
(c) submits to [redacted] any Project Reports specified in Schedule 1 by the agreed report date;  
(d) complies with all relevant [redacted] rules and policies expected of an [redacted] employee operating at an equivalent level;  
(e) complies with any laws, regulations, rules, or guidelines of any government or public authority that may be applicable to the research and any laws, regulations, or guidelines relating to health and safety or ethics; and  
(f) complies with the University’s obligations under the following clauses 5 (Intellectual Property), 6 (Confidential Information), 7 (Thesis and Publications) and 8 (Publicity) as if the Student were a Party to this Agreement.  

3. Health and Safety  

3.11 The Parties must comply with the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (‘HSW Act’), including any regulations, codes and guidance made under the HSW Act.  

3.12 If the Student undertakes the Project at [redacted] premises or controlled site (‘Site’), [redacted] will (without limiting its duties under the HSW Act):  
(a) ensure the health and safety of the Student, as far as reasonably practicable;  
(b) advise the Student as to what is required of them in relation to health and safety, safety equipment, emergency evacuations, the reporting of accidents, hazards, and Notifiable Events (within the meaning of the HSW Act) relating to the Project;  
(c) provide a Site induction and safety briefing to the Student prior to the performance of the Project and copies of the health and safety management plan for the Site that is relevant to the Project;  
(d) provide all necessary personal protection equipment to the Student (if required for the performance of the Project) unless otherwise agreed in writing; and  
(e) ensure that all plant, tools, and equipment used in performing the Project are in a safe and serviceable condition and used in accordance with their operating instructions.  

3.13 If either the University or [redacted] considers that the working conditions or behaviours of either the Student or any other persons on [redacted] Site could cause a risk to health and safety, the University or [redacted] may direct the Student to cease performing the Project immediately and any resulting delays in the Project will not constitute a breach of this Agreement by the Party giving the direction. If the situation is not rectified by the Party creating the risk within a reasonable time, then the other Party will be entitled to immediately terminate this Agreement.  

4. Payment  

4.1 [redacted] will pay the University the Funding (if any) upon receipt of an invoice in accordance with the Payment Schedule in Schedule 1. Any invoice issued under this clause must include:  
4.1.1 sufficient details to enable [redacted] to identify this Agreement, the relevant Project, the particular Milestone which is the subject of the invoice and the amount of Funding payable, and state the [redacted] PO Number;  
4.1.2 all applicable taxable supply information as defined in the Goods and Services Tax Act 1985; and  
4.1.3 any other details reasonably requested by [redacted] from time to time, including any details required under Schedule 1.  

4.2 Subject to the University satisfying the invoicing requirements set out in clause 4.1, payment will be made by [redacted] to the University on the 20th of the month following receipt of invoice(s).  

4.3 [redacted] will only be liable to the University for payment of Funding specified in Schedule 1. Any Funding payable will always be subject to any total or maximum amount of Funding set out in Schedule 1.  

4.4 If a dispute arises in connection with an invoice, [redacted] may withhold the disputed amount (but not any amount which is not in dispute) until the dispute is resolved. [redacted] (acting reasonably) will advise the University of any disputed amount in an invoice prior to the due date for payment and the University will have the opportunity to provide further information to support its claim for payment of such disputed amount within five (5) Working Days for consideration. If the amount claimed is then still in dispute, either Party may refer the matter to dispute resolution in accordance with clause 10.  

4.5 The Funding is exclusive of GST, and the University is responsible for all taxation liabilities, rates, and levies payable in relation to the Funding.

```
7.3 With [redacted] written consent, the Student may publish methods and Results of the Project provided that a copy of any proposed publication or presentation is provided to [redacted] for publication approval by [redacted] at least 60 days (or any other period set out in Schedule 1) in advance of the proposed publication or presentation to a journal, editor or other third party. [redacted] will have 60 days (or any other period set out in Schedule 1) after receipt to object to the proposed publication or presentation. If [redacted] considers that a delay of publication is necessary in order to protect [redacted] commercial interests in the Intellectual Property, [redacted] may require:
   7.3.1 removal of any information (not being scientific information or Results);
   7.3.2 delay publication for a maximum of six months to allow registration or protection of the Intellectual Property;
   7.3.3 where the material contains commercial information for which registration or protection of the Intellectual Property is not possible or appropriate (determined at [redacted] sole discretion), delay publication for a further period as may be agreed between the Parties not exceeding one year, although the University will not unreasonably refuse a request for additional delay in the event that the Intellectual Property might otherwise be lost;

8. Publicity
8.1 The University must not make any communication or announcement at any time to any section of the media relating to or referring to the Project or any Results without the prior written permission of [redacted].
8.2 Each Party will only use the name of another Party or its Affiliates in any publication, advertising, or news release relating to or referring to the Project with the prior written approval of that Party. This approval will not be unreasonably withheld. This does not prevent [redacted] from including the University’s and Student’s names on a list of current research projects or collaborations in [redacted] annual report.

9. Term and Termination
9.1 This Agreement commences on the Start Date and continues until the later of the End Date or completion of the Project to [redacted] reasonable satisfaction unless terminated earlier in accordance with this clause 9.
9.2 Either Party may terminate this Agreement with thirty (30) days’ notice to the other Party in the following circumstances:
   9.2.1 if the Student fails to meet the Course requirements as assessed by the University, so that the Student is no longer eligible to be an enrolled student at the University, in which case this Agreement will be terminated on the withdrawal date of the Student and the University will be liable to return any Funding not disbursed to the Student or utilised as specified at Schedule 1;
   9.2.2 the Student withdraws from their Course, in which case this Agreement will terminate on the withdrawal date of the Student and the University will be liable to return any Funding not disbursed to the Student or utilised as specified at Schedule 1;
   9.2.3 [redacted] may terminate the Agreement due to no fault of the Student, in which case [redacted] undertakes to pay the balance of Funding owing to the Student to the University to support the Student to complete their academic studies (as specified in Schedule 1); or
   9.2.4 in the reasonable opinion of [redacted], the Student’s research is of an unsuitable quality and/or the Student consistently fails to meet the timeframes for Milestones and Project Reports specified in Schedule 1 and the Student has received written warnings from [redacted] and an opportunity to rectify. Such termination will only occur once the University and [redacted] meet with the Student to discuss progress and to determine whether the Student is able to continue the Project.

9.3 In addition to any other right of termination or remedy conferred on the Parties under this Agreement, this Agreement may be terminated at any time and with immediate effect by written notice given by a Party (referred to in this clause as the ‘First Party’) to the other Party if:
   9.3.1 any amount payable by the other Party under this Agreement is more than twenty (20) days overdue and that non-payment is not remedied by the other Party within thirty (30) days of having received notice of non-payment by the First Party;
   9.3.2 the other Party has failed to comply with an earlier written notice given by the First Party specifying a material breach of this Agreement by that other Party and, in the case of a breach which is capable of remedy, requiring that other Party to remedy that breach within thirty (30) days after receipt of that earlier notice;
   9.3.3 the other Party goes into liquidation (other than for a solvent restructuring which has been previously approved in writing by the First Party (which approval may not be unreasonably withheld)) or if a receiver, statutory manager or administrator has been appointed in respect of the other Party or any material part of its assets or if any event analogous in nature has occurred in respect of that other Party under the laws of any relevant jurisdiction;
   9.3.4 the other Party makes any assignment to, or enters into an arrangement for the benefit of, its creditors (other than for the purposes of a solvent restructuring approved by the First Party, for which approval may be withheld by the First Party in its absolute discretion); or
   9.3.5 the other Party becomes unable to pay its debts as they fall due or is presumed pursuant to section 287 of the Companies Act 1993 to be unable to pay its debts.

Page 10 of 17
```

Student Research Agreement  

9.4 Upon termination of this Agreement for whatever reason, such termination will be without prejudice to the rights and remedies of the Parties in respect of any breach of this Agreement by the other Party, where such breach occurred prior to the termination of this Agreement.  

9.5 Expiry or termination of this Agreement for any reason will not affect the validity and enforceability of clauses 5 (Intellectual Property), 6 (Confidential Information), 9 (Term and Termination), 10 (Dispute Resolution), 14 (General) and any other clauses that are clearly intended to survive.  

10. Disputes  

10.1 Subject to any requirement for urgent interlocutory relief, before resorting to external dispute resolution mechanisms (including litigation), the Parties must attempt to settle by negotiation any dispute in relation to this Agreement, including by referring the dispute to any person who may be able to assist in some form of resolution of the dispute. Such an attempt will be required to be made within ten (10) days of either Party giving notice to the other Party that there is a dispute and that it is wished to resolve that dispute by negotiation.  

10.2 If a dispute is not settled by the Parties pursuant to clause 10.1 within twenty (20) days of either Party first sending to the others a notice that a dispute exists, the dispute will be submitted by agreement between the Parties for mediation or expert determination by an appropriate person agreed by the Parties (or if the Parties cannot so agree within twenty-five (25) Working Days of the dispute notice being sent, by the President of the Law Society or their nominee), provided always that neither Party will be obliged to accept as final any expert determination by any expert nominated by the Parties.  

10.3 If the Parties cannot resolve any dispute in accordance with clauses 10.1 and 10.2 then and only then may the Parties proceed to litigation in respect of such dispute, provided that nothing in this clause 10 will prevent either Party from commencing court proceedings for the purposes of seeking urgent interlocutory relief.  

11. Liability  

11.1 The Parties acknowledge that this is an Agreement to carry out research, the Results of which cannot be predicted. Further, the Parties acknowledge that the Project will be undertaken by the Student who may not be experienced in research of any kind and whose work cannot be controlled by the University, the University Supervisor, or the Supervisor. The University gives no warranty or undertaking as to the timing of any part of the Project or the outcome of the Project and makes no warranty as to the suitability of the Project, research, or Results for any purpose.  

11.2 Neither [redacted] nor the University will be liable to the other in the event that the Student fails to complete the Project or withdraws from the Project for any reason, provided that notice of the Student’s withdrawal is provided to [redacted] as soon as practicable after the University is made aware of the same.  

11.3 To the extent permitted by law, the liability of each Party to the other Party in relation to any and all claims in any manner related to this Agreement (whether in contract, tort, including negligence, strict liability, by statute or otherwise) for all events that occur in connection with this Agreement, will be limited in total to the greater of (i) all amounts paid or payable by [redacted] under this Agreement, or [redacted].  

11.4 In no event will either Party be liable (whether in contract, tort, including negligence, strict liability, by statute or otherwise) for any:  

11.4.1 consequential, indirect, incidental, special or punitive damages, or  

11.4.2 loss of profits, business, opportunity, or anticipated savings (whether directly or indirectly arising).  

To avoid doubt, loss that arises in the ordinary course as a natural result of a breach will not be considered “consequential,” “indirect,” “incidental” or “special” for the purposes of this clause 10.11.  

11.5 Nothing in the Agreement excludes or limits either Party’s liability to the other which cannot lawfully be excluded or limited. Each Party’s liability in respect of a claim will be reduced proportionately to the extent that the other Party’s breach, fraud or negligent act or omission caused or contributed to the claim. Each Party agrees that it will use commercially reasonable efforts to minimize any damages it may incur as a result of the other Party’s failure to perform pursuant to this Agreement.  

12. Notices  

12.1 Every notice or communication which a Party gives to the other Party concerning anything relating to this Agreement (a “Notice”) must be in writing and delivered to the Party to whom it is to be sent at the address or email address designated by that Party in writing to the other Party.  

12.2 A notice will be deemed to have been received:  

12.2.1 if delivered by hand, at the time of delivery;  

12.2.2 if sent by prepaid courier, on the 5th Working Day after the date of mailing for domestic post or the 10th Working Day for international post; and  

12.2.3 if sent by email, on the day of transmission, provided the sender does not receive any indication of the failure of, or delay in, delivery within one Working Day after dispatch;  

in each case provided that notices received after 5:00 pm on a Working Day are deemed to have been received on the next Working Day.  

12.3 Until otherwise notified to the other Party in writing, each Party’s address and email address are those specified in the Schedule 1.  

13. Relationship between the Parties  

13.1 Nothing in this Agreement will create, constitute, or evidence any partnership, joint venture, agency, trust, or employer/employee relationship between the Parties.  

13.2 In the performance of the Project, neither Party is authorized or empowered to act as an agent for another.  

Page 11 of 17

Student Research Agreement
Party for any purpose and will not on behalf of the other
Party enter into any contract, warranty, or representation to
any matter. Neither Party will be bound by the acts of the
other Party.
14. General
14.1 No amendment to this Agreement is binding unless in writing
and signed by an authorised representative of both Parties.
14.2 This Agreement constitutes the entire understanding
between the Parties regarding the Project and supersedes
and extinguishes any prior oral or written agreement,
arrangement, statement, correspondence, understanding,
representation and undertaking between the Parties to the
same subject matter dealt with in this Agreement. The
Parties agree that in entering this Agreement, they have not
relied on any representation (including pre-contractual
representations) other than those which are expressly set
out in this Agreement.
14.3 If any term of this Agreement is or becomes illegal, invalid,
or unenforceable in any respect, that term will be read down
to the extent necessary to make it legal, valid, and
enforceable or, if it cannot be read down, deemed severed
from this Agreement. Such change will not affect the
legality, validity, and enforceability of the other provisions of
this Agreement.
14.4 No waiver of any breach or failure to enforce any provision
of this Agreement at any time by either Party will in any way
limit or waive the right of that Party to subsequently enforce
and compel strict compliance with this Agreement.
14.5 This Agreement may not be assigned by a Party to a third
party without the prior written consent of the other Party.
14.6 This Agreement may be signed in counterparts (including
scanned and emailed copies). All counterparts, when taken
together, will constitute one and the same Agreement. A
Party may enter into this Agreement by signing any
counterpart.
14.7 New Zealand laws will apply to this Agreement and the
Parties agree to submit to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of
the New Zealand courts.

clauses.title: 3. Health and Safety
clauses.text:
3. Health and Safety  

3.11 The Parties must comply with the Health and Safety at Work Act 2015 (‘HSW Act’), including any regulations, codes and guidance made under the HSW Act.  

3.12 If the Student undertakes the Project at [redacted] premises or controlled site (‘Site’), [redacted] will (without limiting its duties under the HSW Act):  
(a) ensure the health and safety of the Student, as far as reasonably practicable;  
(b) advise the Student as to what is required of them in relation to health and safety, safety equipment, emergency evacuations, the reporting of accidents, hazards, and Notifiable Events (within the meaning of the HSW Act) relating to the Project;  
(c) provide a Site induction and safety briefing to the Student prior to the performance of the Project and copies of the health and safety management plan for the Site that is relevant to the Project;  
(d) provide all necessary personal protection equipment to the Student (if required for the performance of the Project) unless otherwise agreed in writing; and  
(e) ensure that all plant, tools, and equipment used in performing the Project are in a safe and serviceable condition and used in accordance with their operating instructions.  

3.13 If either the University or [redacted] considers that the working conditions or behaviours of either the Student or any other persons on [redacted] Site could cause a risk to health and safety, the University or [redacted] may direct the Student to cease performing the Project immediately and any resulting delays in the Project will not constitute a breach of this Agreement by the Party giving the direction. If the situation is not rectified by the Party creating the risk within a reasonable time, then the other Party will be entitled to immediately terminate this Agreement.  

4. Payment  

4.1 [redacted] will pay the University the Funding (if any) upon receipt of an invoice in accordance with the Payment Schedule in Schedule 1. Any invoice issued under this clause must include:  
4.1.1 sufficient details to enable [redacted] to identify this Agreement, the relevant Project, the particular Milestone which is the subject of the invoice and the amount of Funding payable, and state the [redacted] PO Number;  
4.1.2 all applicable taxable supply information as defined in the Goods and Services Tax Act 1985; and  
4.1.3 any other details reasonably requested by [redacted] from time to time, including any details required under Schedule 1.  

4.2 Subject to the University satisfying the invoicing requirements set out in clause 4.1, payment will be made by [redacted] to the University on the 20th of the month following receipt of invoice(s).  

4.3 [redacted] will only be liable to the University for payment of Funding specified in Schedule 1. Any Funding payable will always be subject to any total or maximum amount of Funding set out in Schedule 1.  

4.4 If a dispute arises in connection with an invoice, [redacted] may withhold the disputed amount (but not any amount which is not in dispute) until the dispute is resolved. [redacted] (acting reasonably) will advise the University of any disputed amount in an invoice prior to the due date for payment and the University will have the opportunity to provide further information to support its claim for payment of such disputed amount within five (5) Working Days for consideration. If the amount claimed is then still in dispute, either Party may refer the matter to dispute resolution in accordance with clause 10.  

4.5 The Funding is exclusive of GST, and the University is responsible for all taxation liabilities, rates, and levies payable in relation to the Funding.

7.3 With [redacted] written consent, the Student may publish methods and Results of the Project provided that a copy of any proposed publication or presentation is provided to [redacted] for publication approval by [redacted] at least 60 days (or any other period set out in Schedule 1) in advance of the proposed publication or presentation to a journal, editor or other third party. [redacted] will have 60 days (or any other period set out in Schedule 1) after receipt to object to the proposed publication or presentation. If [redacted] considers that a delay of publication is necessary in order to protect [redacted] commercial interests in the Intellectual Property, [redacted] may require:
   7.3.1 removal of any information (not being scientific information or Results);
   7.3.2 delay publication for a maximum of six months to allow registration or protection of the Intellectual Property;
   7.3.3 where the material contains commercial information for which registration or protection of the Intellectual Property is not possible or appropriate (determined at [redacted] sole discretion), delay publication for a further period as may be agreed between the Parties not exceeding one year, although the University will not unreasonably refuse a request for additional delay in the event that the Intellectual Property might otherwise be lost;

8. Publicity
8.1 The University must not make any communication or announcement at any time to any section of the media relating to or referring to the Project or any Results without the prior written permission of [redacted].
8.2 Each Party will only use the name of another Party or its Affiliates in any publication, advertising, or news release relating to or referring to the Project with the prior written approval of that Party. This approval will not be unreasonably withheld. This does not prevent [redacted] from including the University’s and Student’s names on a list of current research projects or collaborations in [redacted] annual report.

9. Term and Termination
9.1 This Agreement commences on the Start Date and continues until the later of the End Date or completion of the Project to [redacted] reasonable satisfaction unless terminated earlier in accordance with this clause 9.
9.2 Either Party may terminate this Agreement with thirty (30) days’ notice to the other Party in the following circumstances:
   9.2.1 if the Student fails to meet the Course requirements as assessed by the University, so that the Student is no longer eligible to be an enrolled student at the University, in which case this Agreement will be terminated on the withdrawal date of the Student and the University will be liable to return any Funding not disbursed to the Student or utilised as specified at Schedule 1;
   9.2.2 the Student withdraws from their Course, in which case this Agreement will terminate on the withdrawal date of the Student and the University will be liable to return any Funding not disbursed to the Student or utilised as specified at Schedule 1;
   9.2.3 [redacted] may terminate the Agreement due to no fault of the Student, in which case [redacted] undertakes to pay the balance of Funding owing to the Student to the University to support the Student to complete their academic studies (as specified in Schedule 1); or
   9.2.4 in the reasonable opinion of [redacted], the Student’s research is of an unsuitable quality and/or the Student consistently fails to meet the timeframes for Milestones and Project Reports specified in Schedule 1 and the Student has received written warnings from [redacted] and an opportunity to rectify. Such termination will only occur once the University and [redacted] meet with the Student to discuss progress and to determine whether the Student is able to continue the Project.

9.3 In addition to any other right of termination or remedy conferred on the Parties under this Agreement, this Agreement may be terminated at any time and with immediate effect by written notice given by a Party (referred to in this clause as the ‘First Party’) to the other Party if:
   9.3.1 any amount payable by the other Party under this Agreement is more than twenty (20) days overdue and that non-payment is not remedied by the other Party within thirty (30) days of having received notice of non-payment by the First Party;
   9.3.2 the other Party has failed to comply with an earlier written notice given by the First Party specifying a material breach of this Agreement by that other Party and, in the case of a breach which is capable of remedy, requiring that other Party to remedy that breach within thirty (30) days after receipt of that earlier notice;
   9.3.3 the other Party goes into liquidation (other than for a solvent restructuring which has been previously approved in writing by the First Party (which approval may not be unreasonably withheld)) or if a receiver, statutory manager or administrator has been appointed in respect of the other Party or any material part of its assets or if any event analogous in nature has occurred in respect of that other Party under the laws of any relevant jurisdiction;
   9.3.4 the other Party makes any assignment to, or enters into an arrangement for the benefit of, its creditors (other than for the purposes of a solvent restructuring approved by the First Party, for which approval may be withheld by the First Party in its absolute discretion); or
   9.3.5 the other Party becomes unable to pay its debts as they fall due or is presumed pursuant to section 287 of the Companies Act 1993 to be unable to pay its debts.

Student Research Agreement  

9.4 Upon termination of this Agreement for whatever reason, such termination will be without prejudice to the rights and remedies of the Parties in respect of any breach of this Agreement by the other Party, where such breach occurred prior to the termination of this Agreement.  

9.5 Expiry or termination of this Agreement for any reason will not affect the validity and enforceability of clauses 5 (Intellectual Property), 6 (Confidential Information), 9 (Term and Termination), 10 (Dispute Resolution), 14 (General) and any other clauses that are clearly intended to survive.  

10. Disputes  

10.1 Subject to any requirement for urgent interlocutory relief, before resorting to external dispute resolution mechanisms (including litigation), the Parties must attempt to settle by negotiation any dispute in relation to this Agreement, including by referring the dispute to any person who may be able to assist in some form of resolution of the dispute. Such an attempt will be required to be made within ten (10) days of either Party giving notice to the other Party that there is a dispute and that it is wished to resolve that dispute by negotiation.  

10.2 If a dispute is not settled by the Parties pursuant to clause 10.1 within twenty (20) days of either Party first sending to the others a notice that a dispute exists, the dispute will be submitted by agreement between the Parties for mediation or expert determination by an appropriate person agreed by the Parties (or if the Parties cannot so agree within twenty-five (25) Working Days of the dispute notice being sent, by the President of the Law Society or their nominee), provided always that neither Party will be obliged to accept as final any expert determination by any expert nominated by the Parties.  

10.3 If the Parties cannot resolve any dispute in accordance with clauses 10.1 and 10.2 then and only then may the Parties proceed to litigation in respect of such dispute, provided that nothing in this clause 10 will prevent either Party from commencing court proceedings for the purposes of seeking urgent interlocutory relief.  

11. Liability  

11.1 The Parties acknowledge that this is an Agreement to carry out research, the Results of which cannot be predicted. Further, the Parties acknowledge that the Project will be undertaken by the Student who may not be experienced in research of any kind and whose work cannot be controlled by the University, the University Supervisor, or the Supervisor. The University gives no warranty or undertaking as to the timing of any part of the Project or the outcome of the Project and makes no warranty as to the suitability of the Project, research, or Results for any purpose.  

11.2 Neither [redacted] nor the University will be liable to the other in the event that the Student fails to complete the Project or withdraws from the Project for any reason, provided that notice of the Student’s withdrawal is provided to [redacted] as soon as practicable after the University is made aware of the same.  

11.3 To the extent permitted by law, the liability of each Party to the other Party in relation to any and all claims in any manner related to this Agreement (whether in contract, tort, including negligence, strict liability, by statute or otherwise) for all events that occur in connection with this Agreement, will be limited in total to the greater of (i) all amounts paid or payable by [redacted] under this Agreement, or [redacted].  

11.4 In no event will either Party be liable (whether in contract, tort, including negligence, strict liability, by statute or otherwise) for any:  

11.4.1 consequential, indirect, incidental, special or punitive damages, or  

11.4.2 loss of profits, business, opportunity, or anticipated savings (whether directly or indirectly arising).  

To avoid doubt, loss that arises in the ordinary course as a natural result of a breach will not be considered “consequential,” “indirect,” “incidental” or “special” for the purposes of this clause 10.11.  

11.5 Nothing in the Agreement excludes or limits either Party’s liability to the other which cannot lawfully be excluded or limited. Each Party’s liability in respect of a claim will be reduced proportionately to the extent that the other Party’s breach, fraud or negligent act or omission caused or contributed to the claim. Each Party agrees that it will use commercially reasonable efforts to minimize any damages it may incur as a result of the other Party’s failure to perform pursuant to this Agreement.  

12. Notices  

12.1 Every notice or communication which a Party gives to the other Party concerning anything relating to this Agreement (a “Notice”) must be in writing and delivered to the Party to whom it is to be sent at the address or email address designated by that Party in writing to the other Party.  

12.2 A notice will be deemed to have been received:  

12.2.1 if delivered by hand, at the time of delivery;  

12.2.2 if sent by prepaid courier, on the 5th Working Day after the date of mailing for domestic post or the 10th Working Day for international post; and  

12.2.3 if sent by email, on the day of transmission, provided the sender does not receive any indication of the failure of, or delay in, delivery within one Working Day after dispatch;  

in each case provided that notices received after 5:00 pm on a Working Day are deemed to have been received on the next Working Day.  

12.3 Until otherwise notified to the other Party in writing, each Party’s address and email address are those specified in the Schedule 1.  

13. Relationship between the Parties  

13.1 Nothing in this Agreement will create, constitute, or evidence any partnership, joint venture, agency, trust, or employer/employee relationship between the Parties.  

13.2 In the performance of the Project, neither Party is authorized or empowered to act as an agent for another.  

Page 11 of 17

Student Research Agreement
Party for any purpose and will not on behalf of the other
Party enter into any contract, warranty, or representation to
any matter. Neither Party will be bound by the acts of the
other Party.
14. General
14.1 No amendment to this Agreement is binding unless in writing
and signed by an authorised representative of both Parties.
14.2 This Agreement constitutes the entire understanding
between the Parties regarding the Project and supersedes
and extinguishes any prior oral or written agreement,
arrangement, statement, correspondence, understanding,
representation and undertaking between the Parties to the
same subject matter dealt with in this Agreement. The
Parties agree that in entering this Agreement, they have not
relied on any representation (including pre-contractual
representations) other than those which are expressly set
out in this Agreement.
14.3 If any term of this Agreement is or becomes illegal, invalid,
or unenforceable in any respect, that term will be read down
to the extent necessary to make it legal, valid, and
enforceable or, if it cannot be read down, deemed severed
from this Agreement. Such change will not affect the
legality, validity, and enforceability of the other provisions of
this Agreement.
14.4 No waiver of any breach or failure to enforce any provision
of this Agreement at any time by either Party will in any way
limit or waive the right of that Party to subsequently enforce

14.5 This Agreement may not be assigned by a Party to a third
party without the prior written consent of the other Party.
14.6 This Agreement may be signed in counterparts (including
scanned and emailed copies). All counterparts, when taken
together, will constitute one and the same Agreement. A
Party may enter into this Agreement by signing any
counterpart.
14.7 New Zealand laws will apply to this Agreement and the
Parties agree to submit to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of
the New Zealand courts.
